content:
First observe that

 abc divides \((ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)\)

if and only if each of \(a,b,c\) divides one of the three factors, namely
 a∣(bc+1), b∣(ca+1), c∣(ab+1).

One checks by a small‐case search that, up to permutation, the only positive integer solutions \((a,b,c)\) are

 (1,1,1),\quad (1,1,2),\quad (1,2,3),\quad (2,3,7).

Counting all their ordered permutations:

• (1,1,1) gives 1 ordered triple,  
• (1,1,2) gives \(\binom3{1}=3\) ordered triples,  
• (1,2,3) gives \(3!=6\),  
• (2,3,7) gives \(3!=6\).  

In total \(1+3+6+6=16\).  Hence the answer is

\(\boxed{16}\).

Counting all their ordered permutations:

• (1,1,2) gives \(\binom3{1}=3\) ordered triples,

• (2,3,7) gives \(3!=6\).

\(\boxed{16}\).
